summary: Mexican novelist, part of the Latin American Boom. Known for The Death of Artemio Cruz, Aura, Terra Nostra, The Old Gringo, Where the Air is Clear, and Christopher Unborn. Friend of Gabriel Garcia Marquez and part of the 'Boom' generation alongside Vargas Llosa and Cortazar.

works.name: The Death of Artemio Cruz
works.description: Novel using three narrative voices (I, you, he) to tell the story of a dying Mexican Revolution veteran. Dedicated to sociologist C. Wright Mills. As Cruz lies on his deathbed, his wife Catalina and daughter Teresa wait to learn the location of his will, while his secretary Padilla plays tapes of his business deals.

clues.clue: Three narrative voices: first person (I), second person (you/tu), third person (he); dying Mexican Revolution veteran Artemio Cruz recalls his life
examples: told from three alternating perspectives: first, second, and third person

clues.clue: Cruz betrays his revolutionary ideals and becomes a corrupt businessman; dies on the date April 10, 1959
examples: a dying former revolutionary turned corrupt businessman

clues.clue: Wife Catalina and daughter Teresa sit by his bedside hoping to learn the location of his will
examples: Catalina and Teresa try to locate the will of the title Civil War veteran while he is being given extreme unction
his daughter Teresa and wife Catalina wait to hear him disclose where he has placed his will

clues.clue: Secretary Padilla plays tape recordings of Cruz's business deals to keep him conscious
examples: Padilla plays a tape of recorded business deals to a dying tycoon
this man is able to stay conscious because Padilla plays for him a tape of his business dealings

clues.clue: Cruz betrays his cellmate Gonzalo Bernal, who is executed by firing squad; then marries Bernal's sister Catalina and inherits Don Gamaliel Bernal's estate
examples: a man watches as Gonzalo Bernal is executed by firing squad, and afterwards gets rich by marrying Bernal's sister
the title character abandons his cellmate to be executed, then marries his cellmate's sister

clues.clue: Cruz's son Lorenzo is sent to manage estates in Cocuya and later dies fighting Franco's forces in Spain
examples: Lorenzo is sent to manage estates in Cocuya by his father just to spite his mother, and he later dies while fighting Franco's forces

clues.clue: Cruz's childhood friend Lunero was a servant; after accidentally shooting his uncle, Cruz fled to Veracruz
examples: this title character's only childhood friend Lunero was the servant of the character's uncle; after accidentally shooting that uncle, this character fled to Veracruz

clues.clue: Dedicated to American sociologist C. Wright Mills, author of The Power Elite
examples: Carlos Fuentes dedicated The Death of Artemio Cruz to this American sociologist

clues.clue: Citizen Kane cited as inspiration for the novel's structure
examples: Citizen Kane inspired a novel about the deathbed of a corrupt politician

works.name: Aura
works.description: Novella written entirely in second person about a young historian Felipe Montero hired by the 109-year-old Senora Consuelo to write the memoirs of her dead husband. Felipe falls in love with the green-eyed title character, who turns out to be a magically created younger version of Consuelo.

clues.clue: Written entirely in second-person 'you' (tu); young historian Felipe Montero catalogues memoirs for elderly widow Consuelo; Aura is Consuelo's young double
examples: written in second person 'you'
Felipe Montero is hired to catalogue memoirs

clues.clue: Consuelo is 109 years old; she conducted experiments with magical herbs; her dead husband had reactionary opinions on Republicanism, Boulanger, and Dreyfus
examples: the protagonist poring over yellowed papers detailing their author's reactionary opinions on Republicanism, Boulanger, and Dreyfus, which give way to notes on experiments that writer's wife conducted with magical herbs

clues.clue: A rabbit named Saga and a group of cats that Aura tortures live in the rat-infested, perpetually dark house
examples: a rabbit named Saga and a group of cats that the title character tortures live in the rat-infested, perpetually dark house

clues.clue: Aura has green eyes; Montero transforms into General Llorente while Aura becomes old widow Consuelo
examples: the title green-eyed woman transforms into the old widow Consuelo
Montero transform into General Llorente while the title character becomes an old widow named Consuelo

works.name: The Old Gringo
works.description: Novel fictionalizing Ambrose Bierce's disappearance in Mexico during the Mexican Revolution. The title character, nicknamed the 'Indiana General,' joins Tomas Arroyo's forces, falls in love with American governess Harriet Winslow, and is killed after burning land-granting documents signed by the King of Spain. Jane Fonda and Gregory Peck starred in a film adaptation.

clues.clue: Title character is based on Ambrose Bierce, who disappeared in Mexico; fictionalizes Bierce's death during the Mexican Revolution
examples: Ambrose Bierce inspired the elderly title character of what Carlos Fuentes novel
this novel fictionalizes Ambrose Bierce's disappearance in Mexico

clues.clue: American governess Harriet Winslow goes to Mexico to tutor the Miranda children; develops a relationship with the title character
examples: Harriet Winslow is hired as a governess
Harriet Winslow fights to have this character buried in Arlington Cemetery

clues.clue: Title character shoots a hole through a silver coin to prove his marksmanship; carries a copy of Don Quixote and a Colt .44
examples: this character shoots through a silver coin while carrying a copy of Don Quixote
shoots a hole through a silver coin and carries a copy of Don Quixote

clues.clue: Title character is killed (shot in the back) after burning false property papers/land-granting documents from the King of Spain; Tomas Arroyo is then executed by Pancho Villa
examples: this character is shot in the back after some false property papers from the King of Spain are burned
a man who 'came here to die' is killed for burning land-granting documents, leading to the execution of Tomas Arroyo

clues.clue: Soldiers leave a ballroom of mirrors intact after gazing at their reflections; the mirrored ballroom is all that remains of the Miranda estate
examples: soldiers leave a ballroom intact after gazing at their reflections in its giant mirrors
a mirrored ballroom is all that remains of an estate

clues.clue: Title character nicknamed the 'Indiana General' after his Civil War service; also partly based on William Benton
examples: known as the 'Indiana General' out of reference to his Civil War service

clues.clue: Title character's corpse is buried, then dug up and executed by firing squad to prevent a diplomatic incident; Harriet Winslow has him buried in Arlington Cemetery
examples: after he is buried, he is shot again by an actual firing squad to prevent a diplomatic incident
Harriet Winslow fights to have this character buried in Arlington Cemetery

clues.clue: Jane Fonda earned a Razzie nomination; Gregory Peck played the title character in the film adaptation
examples: Jane Fonda earned a Razzie nomination for starring in a film titled for one of these people where an aged Gregory Peck is killed

clues.clue: Harriet's father left his family to live with a Cuban woman during the American invasion of Cuba
examples: Harriet's father was believed to have been killed in combat during the US's intervention in Cuba

works.name: Terra Nostra
works.description: Massive 800-page novel divided into sections 'Old World,' 'New World,' and 'Next World.' Characters like the student Ludovico and peasant girl/witch Celestina are reborn in different historical eras, first appearing during Philip II's building of El Escorial. Features Don Quixote and Hieronymus Bosch among its expansive cast. Opens in a future Paris where the Arc de Triomphe is made of sand.

clues.clue: Massive 800-page novel divided into sections Old World, New World, and Next World; characters reborn across historical eras; features Philip II and El Escorial
examples: in this 800-page novel divided into the sections Old World, New World, and Next World

clues.clue: Characters include student Ludovico and peasant girl/witch Celestina who are reborn in different eras; Don Quixote and Hieronymus Bosch appear in the expansive cast
examples: this novel, whose expansive dramatis personae includes Don Quixote and Hieronymus Bosch

clues.clue: Opens in a future Paris where the Arc de Triomphe is made of sand and the Eiffel Tower is a zoo; a 90-year-old concierge gives birth to a baby with six toes and a wine-red cross on his back
examples: opens in a future Paris where the Arc de Triomphe is made of sand and the Eiffel Tower is a zoo

clues.clue: The Garden of Earthly Delights appears in a mirror in El Senor's palace, symbolizing the novel's three-part structure; hundreds of women give birth along the Seine in 'The Old World' section
examples: The Garden of Earthly Delights appears in a mirror in El Senor's palace

clues.clue: Title means 'our earth'
examples: this epic novel by Carlos Fuentes, whose title means 'our earth'

works.name: Where the Air is Clear
works.description: Fuentes's 1958 debut novel, set in post-revolutionary Mexico City. Centers on the enigmatic Ixca Cienfuegos and follows business tycoon Federico Robles, who turns his back on his revolutionary past. Takes its title from a line from Alfonso Reyes's Vision de Anahuac. Published by Dalkey Archive Press in English.

clues.clue: 1958 debut novel set in Mexico City; centers on enigmatic Ixca Cienfuegos; follows business tycoon Federico Robles in post-revolutionary Mexico
examples: this Fuentes novel centers on Ixca Cienfuegos in a post-revolutionary Mexico City
opens with Ixca Cienfuegos declaring 'in Mexico City there is never tragedy but only outrage'

clues.clue: Former revolutionary Federico Robles marries Norma Larragoiti; writer Rodrigo Pola is another character
examples: the former revolutionary Federico Robles marries Norma Laragoiti in the years following the Mexican Revolution
Two characters in this work are the writer Rodrigo Pola, and a woman trying to improve her social status, Norma Larragoiti

clues.clue: Title comes from a line from Alfonso Reyes's Vision de Anahuac (La region mas transparente)
examples: takes its name from a line from Vision de Anahuac

works.name: Christopher Unborn
works.description: Novel borrowing its structure from Tristram Shandy, narrated by an unborn fetus. Angel and Angeles scheme to conceive a child born exactly at midnight on October 12, 1992 -- the 500th anniversary of Columbus's discovery of the Americas -- to win a government prize.

clues.clue: Narrated by an unborn fetus; Angel and Angeles scheme to conceive a child born exactly on October 12, 1992 (500th anniversary of Columbus's discovery) to win a government prize
examples: Angel and Angeles scheme to have a child born at exactly midnight of October 12, 1992
a young couple scheme to win a government prize by conceiving a son exactly on the anniversary

clues.clue: Structure borrowed from Tristram Shandy; described as the last of Fuentes's major novels
examples: this last of Fuentes's major novels borrows its structure from Tristram Shandy

works.name: General / Biographical

clues.clue: Mexican author; contemporary of Octavio Paz; born 1928, died 2012

comprehensive_summary:
Carlos Fuentes (1928-2012) was a Mexican novelist and a key figure in the Latin American literary Boom alongside Garcia Marquez, Vargas Llosa, and Cortazar. He was a contemporary of Octavio Paz.

The Death of Artemio Cruz, dedicated to sociologist C. Wright Mills, uses three alternating narrative voices -- first person (I), second person (you/tu), and third person (he) -- inspired partly by Citizen Kane. The dying businessman Artemio Cruz, whose wife Catalina and daughter Teresa wait by his bedside hoping to learn the location of his will, reflects on his life while his secretary Padilla plays tapes of his business deals. Cruz betrayed his cellmate Gonzalo Bernal (who was executed by firing squad), then married Bernal's sister Catalina and inherited Don Gamaliel Bernal's estate. His son Lorenzo was sent to manage estates in Cocuya and later died fighting Franco's forces in Spain. Cruz's childhood friend was Lunero; after accidentally shooting his uncle, Cruz fled to Veracruz.

Aura is a novella written entirely in second person, in which the young historian Felipe Montero is hired by the 109-year-old Senora Consuelo to catalogue the memoirs of her dead husband (whose papers reveal reactionary opinions on Republicanism, Boulanger, and Dreyfus, and notes on Consuelo's experiments with magical herbs). The green-eyed title character turns out to be a magically created younger version of Consuelo. A rabbit named Saga and cats live in the rat-infested, perpetually dark house. Montero transforms into General Llorente while Aura becomes old Consuelo.

The Old Gringo fictionalizes Ambrose Bierce's disappearance in Mexico during the Mexican Revolution. The title character, nicknamed the 'Indiana General' (also partly based on William Benton), carries a copy of Don Quixote and a Colt .44, and proves his marksmanship by shooting a hole through a silver coin. He joins Tomas Arroyo's forces, falls in love with American governess Harriet Winslow (who came to tutor the Miranda children), and is killed after burning false land-granting documents signed by the King of Spain. Arroyo is then executed by Pancho Villa. The gringo's corpse is dug up and shot by firing squad to prevent a diplomatic incident; Winslow has him buried in Arlington Cemetery. Soldiers leave a ballroom of mirrors intact after gazing at their reflections. Harriet's father had disappeared during the American intervention in Cuba. Jane Fonda and Gregory Peck starred in the film adaptation.

Terra Nostra is a massive 800-page novel divided into 'Old World,' 'New World,' and 'Next World.' Characters like the student Ludovico and peasant girl/witch Celestina are reborn across historical eras, first appearing during Philip II's building of El Escorial. The expansive cast includes Don Quixote and Hieronymus Bosch. The Garden of Earthly Delights appears in a mirror in El Senor's palace, symbolizing the novel's three-part structure. The novel opens in a future Paris where the Arc de Triomphe is made of sand and the Eiffel Tower is a zoo. Its title means 'our earth.'

Where the Air is Clear (La region mas transparente) is Fuentes's 1958 debut novel, set in post-revolutionary Mexico City. It centers on the enigmatic Ixca Cienfuegos and follows business tycoon Federico Robles, who married Norma Larragoiti. The title comes from Alfonso Reyes's Vision de Anahuac.

Christopher Unborn borrows its structure from Tristram Shandy and is narrated by an unborn fetus. Angel and Angeles scheme to have a child born at midnight on October 12, 1992 -- the 500th anniversary of Columbus's discovery of the Americas -- to win a government prize.

Other works include 'Chac Mool,' a short story from the collection Burnt Water in which Filiberto buys a chac-mool (Mesoamerican reclining statue) that comes to life, floods his home, and kills him; Nietzsche on His Balcony, Fuentes's last novel in which Nietzsche is reincarnated once a year for 24 hours; and The Hydra Head. Fuentes also wrote the introduction to Frida Kahlo's diary.
